civil_war_quotes.author: Robert E. Lee
civil_war_quotes.text: It is well that war is so terrible, or we should get too fond of it.
civil_war_quotes.context: Spoken to James Longstreet during the Battle of Fredericksburg, 1862.

civil_war_quotes.author: Thomas 'Stonewall' Jackson
civil_war_quotes.text: Let us cross over the river, and rest under the shade of the trees.

civil_war_quotes.author: James Longstreet
civil_war_quotes.text: I do not think the day is far distant when we shall be as good friends as ever.

civil_war_quotes.author: J.E.B. Stuart
civil_war_quotes.text: I had rather die a thousand deaths than betray my country or any part of it.
civil_war_quotes.context: Written in a letter to his wife, Flora Stuart.

civil_war_quotes.author: Patrick Cleburne
civil_war_quotes.text: If this nation dies, the monument expected to commemorate its glorious institutions will be a monument to our failure.
civil_war_quotes.context: From his 1864 memorandum to the Army of Tennessee.

civil_war_quotes.author: Nathan Bedford Forrest
civil_war_quotes.text: Whenever you meet the enemy no matter how few there are of you or how many of them, show fight.
civil_war_quotes.context: Attributed tactical philosophy regarding aggressive engagement.

civil_war_quotes.author: Joseph E. Johnston
civil_war_quotes.text: An army is simply a vast community on legs... here one day, and the next day gone.
civil_war_quotes.context: Reflecting on the nature of logistics and movement in the field.

civil_war_quotes.author: P.G.T. Beauregard
civil_war_quotes.text: No people ever yet lost their liberties who had the courage to defend them.
civil_war_quotes.context: Proclamation to the people of Louisiana, 1862.

civil_war_quotes.author: Richard S. Ewell
civil_war_quotes.text: It is better to be a dead lion than a living dog.
civil_war_quotes.context: Attributed remark regarding the spirit of the infantry.

civil_war_quotes.author: Thomas 'Stonewall' Jackson
civil_war_quotes.text: My duty is to obey orders.
civil_war_quotes.context: Jackson's core military philosophy during the Valley Campaign.

civil_war_quotes.author: Robert E. Lee
civil_war_quotes.text: Duty, then, is the sublimest word in our language. Do your duty in all things. You cannot do more, you should never wish to do less.
civil_war_quotes.context: Letter to his son, G.W. Custis Lee.

civil_war_quotes.author: James Longstreet
civil_war_quotes.text: The ground was covered with the dead and dying... it was a scene that I never want to see again.
civil_war_quotes.context: Account of the aftermath of the Battle of Antietam.

civil_war_quotes.author: John Bell Hood
civil_war_quotes.text: The time for talking has passed; the time for fighting has come.
civil_war_quotes.context: Spoken during the defense of Atlanta, 1864.

civil_war_quotes.author: A.P. Hill
civil_war_quotes.text: Tell Hill he must come up!
civil_war_quotes.context: Both Lee and Jackson reportedly called for Hill on their deathbeds, reflecting his reliability.

civil_war_quotes.author: Patrick Cleburne
civil_war_quotes.text: Every man should be a soldier. This is not the time to be a gentleman.
civil_war_quotes.context: Attributed to Cleburne regarding the total commitment required for war.
